audit_items.proof_trace: AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet() explicitly returns NULL on two failure paths (random-private.h:67, :73) but its return value at threshold.c:2075 is never NULL-checked before the first dereference at line 2078 (random_info[0] under OpenMP) and line 2127 (random_info[id] in the pixel loop). No enforcement mechanism exists: the same unchecked pattern is repeated across fx.c, statistic.c, and effect.c, confirming systemic missing NULL checks rather than intentional design.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker submits a crafted image (or floods the server with concurrent image requests) to exhaust heap memory; when AcquireRandomInfo() or AcquireQuantumMemory fails inside AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet(), the function returns NULL, and the subsequent dereference of random_info[0] or random_info[id] causes a NULL pointer dereference crash (DoS).
